meta.purpose: Draft Modbus register map for the IEIO-100 Modbus Remote IO family. This file is intended for engineering review, firmware implementation planning and later validation test cases.
meta.important_note: This is not a confirmed production specification. Do not publish exact register addresses, electrical ratings, accuracy or timing claims on public product pages until firmware and prototype validation pass.

scope.positioning: Modbus Remote IO module family for distributed DI, DO/relay, AI and AO expansion.
optional_future_protocols: Modbus TCP for a future Ethernet Remote IO variant

first_generation_variants.description: 8-channel digital output or relay output module. Final output type and rating require hardware validation.
4-channel analog input module. Current and voltage modes require hardware validation.
4-channel analog output module. Output mode, resolution and load capability require hardware validation.

addressing_convention.protocol_addressing: Modbus protocol addresses are zero-based offsets in the request frame.
addressing_convention.documentation_addressing: Documentation display addresses use common Modbus reference notation such as 00001, 10001, 30001 and 40001.
addressing_convention.conversion_rule: Protocol offset = documentation display address minus base address. Example: display register 40001 uses protocol offset 0 for FC03/FC06/FC16.

address_spaces.usage: Writable DO/relay command and state bits.
Read-only AI values, device status and device information.
Writable AO setpoints and device or IO configuration.

supported_function_codes.name: Read Coils
supported_function_codes.usage: Read DO/relay output states on DO-capable variants.

supported_function_codes.name: Read Discrete Inputs
supported_function_codes.usage: Read DI states on DI-capable variants.

supported_function_codes.name: Read Holding Registers
supported_function_codes.usage: Read AO setpoints and configuration registers.

supported_function_codes.name: Read Input Registers
supported_function_codes.usage: Read AI values, status registers and device information.

supported_function_codes.name: Write Single Coil
supported_function_codes.usage: Write one DO/relay output on DO-capable variants.

supported_function_codes.name: Write Single Holding Register
supported_function_codes.usage: Write one AO setpoint or configuration register.

supported_function_codes.name: Write Multiple Coils
supported_function_codes.usage: Write multiple DO/relay outputs on DO-capable variants.

supported_function_codes.name: Write Multiple Holding Registers
supported_function_codes.usage: Write multiple AO setpoints or configuration registers.

unsupported_baseline_function_codes.name: Read Exception Status
unsupported_baseline_function_codes.reason: Not required for baseline Remote IO behavior.

data_encoding.word_order_for_32bit_values: High word first by default; configurable word order can be considered later if customer PLC compatibility requires it.
data_encoding.signed_values: Use signed 16-bit two's complement only where the register explicitly states int16.
data_encoding.bitmask_values: Bit 0 represents channel 1 unless otherwise specified.

ai_raw_unit.current_mode: 0.001 mA. Example: 4000 means 4.000 mA; 20000 means 20.000 mA.
ai_raw_unit.voltage_mode: 0.001 V. Example: 0 means 0.000 V; 10000 means 10.000 V.

coil_map.name: DO/relay output command state
coil_map.notes: For IEIO-100-MD44, only channels 1-4 are available. Accessing unavailable channels must return Modbus exception 02.

discrete_input_map.name: DI channel state
discrete_input_map.notes: For IEIO-100-MD44, only channels 1-4 are available. Input polarity and wet/dry contact behavior require hardware validation.

input_register_map.name: AI raw value
input_register_map.unit: 0.001 mA or 0.001 V based on channel mode
input_register_map.notes: For IEIO-100-MA44, all four AI channels are available. AI mode and accuracy require hardware validation.

input_register_map.name: Register map version
input_register_map.notes: Firmware should return 10 for version 0.1.0 draft after implementation agreement.

holding_register_map.name: AO output setpoint
holding_register_map.unit: 0.001 mA or 0.001 V based on channel mode
holding_register_map.notes: Final output range, resolution and load capability require hardware validation.

holding_register_map.name: Modbus slave address
holding_register_map.notes: New address takes effect only after apply command or reboot.

holding_register_map.name: Apply communication configuration
holding_register_map.notes: Firmware should reject all other values with exception 03.

holding_register_map.name: Device reboot command
holding_register_map.notes: Use only for engineering/test tools; public documentation should include warning text.

holding_register_map.name: DI channel mode
holding_register_map.notes: Wet/dry contact and pulse mode availability require hardware and firmware validation.

holding_register_map.name: DO/relay failsafe state
holding_register_map.notes: Failsafe trigger condition requires firmware definition.

holding_register_map.name: AI channel mode
holding_register_map.notes: Final channel mode options depend on analog front-end hardware.

holding_register_map.name: AO channel mode
holding_register_map.notes: Final channel mode options depend on analog output hardware.

holding_register_map.name: AO failsafe value
holding_register_map.unit: 0.001 mA or 0.001 V based on channel mode
holding_register_map.notes: Value must be validated against AO channel range.

variant_applicability_rules.rule: If a register or coil belongs to an IO channel not present on the model, firmware must return Modbus exception 02 Illegal Data Address.
If a function code is unsupported by the firmware, return Modbus exception 01 Illegal Function.
If a write value is outside the defined enum or valid range, return Modbus exception 03 Illegal Data Value.
Reserved addresses should return exception 02 until they are formally assigned in a future register map version.

static_review: Confirm address ranges do not overlap within each Modbus address space.
Confirm each first-generation variant has a clear applicable register subset.
Confirm display addresses and zero-based protocol offsets are both documented.
Confirm unavailable channel behavior is deterministic.
firmware_validation: Verify every supported function code with normal and boundary values.
Verify invalid write values return exception 03.
Verify communication settings persist after reboot when apply command is used.
Verify output failsafe behavior after communication timeout and power cycle.
prototype_validation: Verify DI state changes with actual input wiring.
Verify DO/relay output behavior with actual load within validated rating.
Verify AI reading accuracy and stability using calibrated current/voltage source.
Verify AO output accuracy and load behavior using calibrated meter/load.
Verify RS485 multi-drop communication stability with at least 3 nodes on the bus.
recommended_tools: PLC or SCADA master used by target customers
Calibrated current/voltage source for AI validation
Calibrated multimeter or data logger for AO validation

validation_test_cases.title: Read DI channels
validation_test_cases.procedure: Use FC02 to read display addresses 10001-10008 while toggling each physical DI channel.
validation_test_cases.expected_result: Returned bit states match physical input states; unavailable channels on MD44 return exception 02 if addressed beyond channel 4.

validation_test_cases.title: Write DO/relay channels
validation_test_cases.procedure: Use FC05 and FC15 to write display addresses 00001-00008, then use FC01 to read back states.
validation_test_cases.expected_result: Physical outputs follow command values; readback matches output state; unavailable channels on MD44 return exception 02 if addressed beyond channel 4.

validation_test_cases.title: Read AI raw values
validation_test_cases.procedure: Apply known current or voltage values and read display addresses 30001-30004 using FC04.
validation_test_cases.expected_result: Raw values match the defined 0.001 mA or 0.001 V unit within validated engineering tolerance.

validation_test_cases.title: Write and read AO setpoints
validation_test_cases.procedure: Use FC06 and FC16 to write display addresses 40001-40004, then measure physical AO output.
validation_test_cases.expected_result: Physical AO output matches setpoint within validated engineering tolerance; out-of-range values return exception 03.

validation_test_cases.title: Invalid address behavior
validation_test_cases.procedure: Read and write reserved or unavailable addresses in each address space.
validation_test_cases.expected_result: Firmware returns Modbus exception 02 Illegal Data Address.

validation_test_cases.title: Invalid value behavior
validation_test_cases.procedure: Write invalid enum values to baud rate, parity, IO mode and command registers.
validation_test_cases.expected_result: Firmware returns Modbus exception 03 Illegal Data Value and does not change existing configuration.

validation_test_cases.title: Communication configuration apply
validation_test_cases.procedure: Write new slave address, baud rate or parity, then write 0xA55A to display register 41006.
validation_test_cases.expected_result: New communication parameters take effect according to firmware design and persist after reboot.

validation_test_cases.title: Power cycle persistence
validation_test_cases.procedure: Configure communication and IO mode registers, reboot device, then read back configuration.
validation_test_cases.expected_result: Persistent configuration remains stored; volatile output states follow defined startup or failsafe behavior.

validation_test_cases.title: RS485 multi-drop stability
validation_test_cases.procedure: Run IEIO-100 with at least two additional Modbus RTU slave devices on the same RS485 bus and poll continuously.
validation_test_cases.expected_result: No address conflict; stable polling; communication error counter remains within engineering acceptance threshold.

validation_test_cases.title: Register map version check
validation_test_cases.expected_result: Firmware returns the agreed register map version for traceability.

allowed_before_validation: Designed as a Modbus RTU Remote IO family.
Planned DI8, DO8/relay, AI4 and AO4 variants.
not_allowed_before_validation: Guaranteed PLC compatibility with a named PLC brand.

next_actions: Engineering review of address ranges, function codes and variant applicability.
Decide whether DI mode and AI/AO mode are hardware-configurable or fixed per SKU.
Decide DO output type for IEIO-100-DO8: transistor output, relay output or separate variants.
Implement firmware simulator or prototype firmware based on this register map.
Run MR-001 through MR-010 and record evidence in first-product-capability-validation-checklist.json.
